summary:
Finasteride decreases prostate size by targeting 5-alpha reductase, converting less testosterone into DHT; multiple studies show significant reductions in prostate volume after prolonged use compared to alternatives such as alpha-blockers or minimally-invasive techniques. ``` Summary: 
Finasteride shrinks the prostate by blocking 5-alpha reductase, leading to reduced tissue growth, supported by extensive research demonstrating effective shrinkage not seen with other treatment options. ```

The summary focuses only on the critical aspects related to how finasteride works clinically and its effectiveness based on available data.
